content:
anchors: "<" | "401"
ctx: auth_mw expiry
rule: expMs < nowMs - 30000 => 401
need: allow skew30 only
issue: skew30 boundary refresh_loop
deliver: min_fix reg_test

We will use a PostgreSQL database. The team has 4 months to deliver, and there are 6 engineers on the team. After the release, we will split into a separate team. The operations team will be pt_devops. We anticipate modest traffic.

This change introduces a risk where users can spoof their ID by setting the `x-user-id` header.
Verification steps should include testing with and without the `x-user-id` header to ensure proper authentication and authorization.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: bc(exp_lt_no_skew)
C: sec ∧ keep_401
H: skew30_fix
P: edge(-31s_401) → reg_t
V: edge(-30s_200) ∧ exp_lt_no_skew
R: ! edge(-30s_200)
A: skew30_fix

[AUDIT]
Goal: backward compatibility(exp < now without grace window).
Constraints: security and keep 401 behavior.
Hypothesis: 30-second grace window fix.
Plan: edge(-31s_401) then regression test.
Verify: edge(-30s_200) and exp < now without grace window.
Risk: high-risk edge(-30s_200).
Answer target: 30-second grace window fix.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: decide(mod_monolith)
C: team(6) ∧ ddl("4 months") ∧ store("PostgreSQL") ∧ traffic(modest) ∧ split(post_release)
H: ops(pt_devops) ⇒ prefer(fast_ship)
P: deliver(default_arch) → monolith_now
R: !deliver(short_why) ∧ !ops
A: mod_monolith

[AUDIT]
Goal: decide(modular_monolith).
Constraints: team(6) and deadline("4 months") and store("PostgreSQL") and traffic(modest) and split(post_release).
Hypothesis: ops(pt_devops) implies prefer(fast shipping).
Plan: deliver(default_arch) then ship modular_monolith now.
Risk: high-risk deliver(short_why) and high-risk ops.
Answer target: modular_monolith.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: review(patch)
H: authz_bypass ∧ sess_only ⇒ data_tamper
P: spoof(x-user-id) → hdr_override
V: hdr_override ∧ keep_401
R: ! drop_hdr ∧ ! spoof_denied
A: authz_bypass

[AUDIT]
Goal: review(patch).
Hypothesis: authorization bypass and verified session auth only implies data_tamper.
Plan: spoof(x-user-id) then header override.
Verify: header override and keep 401 behavior.
Risk: high-risk drop the header path and high-risk spoofed requests denied.
Answer target: authorization bypass.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: refactor(loadUser)
C: async_await ∧ minimal_async_await
P: missing_id_before_db → db.findUser(id)
V: not_found_before_audit ∧ audit.log
R: !promisify(db.findUser) ∧ !promisify(audit.log)
A: one_next(err) ∧ await(cache.set)

[AUDIT]
Goal: refactor(loadUser).
Constraints: async/await and minimal async/await migration.
Plan: missing id check before database call then db.findUser(id).
Verify: not found check before audit and audit.log.
Risk: high-risk promisify(db.findUser) and high-risk promisify(audit.log).
Answer target: one_next(err) and await(cache.set).
